memoryArtifacts.headline: Caution: See note from "StevenMartin61" below. Ancestry of Lawrence Williams, complied by Cornelia Bartow Williams, Chicago 1915
Clifford Stott correction in TAG(October 1995): 252. Gustave Anjou's error that gave Thomas Lothrop of Yorkshire INCORREC wife, Mary Salte.
memoryArtifacts.description:
There were 2 different men named Thomas Lothrop. Thomas Lothrop of Cherry Burton and Etton, Yorkshire; and Thomas Lathrop of Yoxall, Staffordshire. They were DIFFERENT men. Thomas Lathrop of Yoxall, Staffordshire was the husband of Mary Salte. Thomas Lothrop of Yorkshire md. 2) Maud ____? (however, often called Mary ___? in print.) See see  TAG article at americanancestors.org
https://www.americanancestors.org/databases/american-genealogist-the/image?volumeId=13130&pageName=250&rId=234371181
Please also refer to the Lo-Lathrop Memoir.

popularStory.description: Thomas Lathrop was born in June 1536 in Cherry Burton, Yorkshire, England.  He was the oldest child of Robert Lathrop and Ellen Aston.  His Mother was about 17 when he was born.  He had four younge …

nameMeanings.name: Lothrop
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Lowthorpe in East Yorkshire, named with the Old Norse personal name Logi or Lági + thorp ‘outlying farmstead’.

History: In 1634 the name was brought to North America by the Rev. John Lathrop (born 1584 in Etton, Yorkshire, England), a Puritan preacher fleeing religious persecution. He arrived in Plymouth Colony and lived in Scituate, MA, until 1639, then moved to Barnstable, MA, where his Bible can still be seen.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Lothrop was born in 1536, in Cherry Burton, Yorkshire, England, his father, Robert Lowthrop, was 23 and his mother, Ellen, was 19. He married Elizabeth Clarke on 27 November 1558, in South Cave, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 5 daughters. He died before 9 October 1606, in Etton, Yorkshire, England, and was buried in Etton, Yorkshire, England.

fr.Etton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdo: Etton, Yorkshire East Riding, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Lothrop est né en 1536, à Cherry Burton, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Robert Lowthrop, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Ellen, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Clarke le 27 novembre 1558, à South Cave, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé avant 9 octobre 1606, à Etton, Everwyck, Angleterre, et a été enterré à Etton, Everwyck, Angleterre.

Oktober 1606 in Etton, Yorkshire, England und wurde in Etton, Yorkshire, England begraben.

ko.Etton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdo: 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 이스트 리딩 턴
ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Lothrop(이)가 1536년 영국 요르크실르 처리 버턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Lowthrop(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Ellen(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1558년 11월 27일 영국 요르크실르 소우스 카브에서 Elizabeth Clarke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1606년 10월 9일 이전 영국 요르크실르 턴에서 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 턴에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Clarke em 27 de novembro de 1558, em South Cave, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 5 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Lothrop родился в 1536 г. в местности чирри буртон, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Lowthrop было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Ellen, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Clarke 27 ноября 1558 г. в местности соут кав, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 5 дочерей. Он умер перед 9 октябрь 1606 г. в местности эттон, еркшир, Англия и был похоронен в местности эттон, еркшир, Англия.
